# DJC Marketing — DakJen Creative LLC

DJC Marketing is a new, independently owned lease-up marketing company built specifically for affordable and workforce housing — a niche that established marketing agencies consistently underserve. We are founder-led, affordable, and deeply embedded in the development world in a way that most marketing firms simply are not.

## Who We Are

DJC Marketing was founded by Dakotah Jennifer, a young entrepreneur who spent over five years working inside the real estate development industry before launching her own firm. She didn't come to affordable housing marketing from the outside — she came from within the process itself, working alongside developers, property managers, investors, and community organizations from project launch through occupancy. That experience gave her a fluency in this space that no traditional marketing background can replicate.

DJC is a new company, but it is not inexperienced. Dakotah built this firm on the back of years of hands-on development work, and the results from our first major engagement — a 116-unit mixed-income affordable housing development at 9410 Hough Avenue in Cleveland, Ohio — speak for themselves: 400 inquiries in the first 48 hours, 470+ total applications, and a 36.2% application success rate that is 13.7 points above the industry average.

## Our Place in the Field

Lease-up marketing for affordable housing is a highly specialized niche. Most marketing agencies treat it like any other multifamily campaign. They don't understand the regulatory environment, the Section 8 and HCVP dynamics, the community trust challenges, or the pressure that delayed stabilization puts on developers and investors. DJC was built to fill that gap.

We sit at the intersection of marketing, community engagement, and development-sector expertise. We know how affordable housing deals are structured, what investors need to see, and how to communicate with prospective residents in communities where trust has to be earned — not assumed. That position is rare, and it is the foundation everything we do is built on.

## What Makes Us Different

Most lease-up marketing firms are either large agencies that treat affordable housing as a side product, or small shops without the development-sector knowledge to navigate the complexity. DJC is neither. We are a focused, affordable, founder-led firm that exists specifically for this work — and because Dakotah runs every engagement personally, clients get senior-level attention without enterprise pricing.

We are accessible to developers at a range of budget levels. We believe that smaller developers and first-time affordable housing projects deserve the same quality of marketing support that large institutional developers receive — and we price and structure our work accordingly.

## Founder

**Dakotah Jennifer** — Founder & Project Lead

Dakotah Jennifer is a young entrepreneur with over five years of experience inside the real estate development industry. She has worked alongside developers, property managers, investors, and community organizations across the full arc of affordable housing projects — from pre-development planning through lease-up and stabilization. She launched DJC Marketing because she saw a clear gap: the marketing firms available to affordable housing developers either didn't understand the space or were too expensive and too generalist to serve it well.

Dakotah leads every DJC engagement personally. She is the strategist, the analyst, the client contact, and the person accountable for results. That founder-led model is a deliberate choice — it keeps the work sharp and the accountability real.

## Proven Results

At 9410 Hough Avenue, Cleveland, OH — a 116-unit mixed-income affordable housing development — DJC delivered:
- 400 inquiries in the first 48 hours of launch
- 150 applications opened in the first 48 hours
- 470+ total applications generated
- 36.2% application success rate (industry average is approximately 22.5%)
- Over 20% reapplication rate driven by active applicant follow-up
- 18.8% strong candidate rate, nearly double the industry benchmark
